pattern: Each scrambled token carries its own 1-indexed position as a trailing digit, so placing every word by its embedded rank and dropping the digit rebuilds the original order.
hints: Every word ends in a single digit telling you exactly where it belongs.
Split on spaces, read the final character of each token as its target slot, and strip it off.
Create an array sized to the word count, drop each stripped word at index (digit - 1), then join with single spaces.

edge_inputs.description: words already appear in reverse order

input: c3 i9 a1 b2 d4 e5 f6 g7 h8

edge_inputs.description: single word with its position digit

stress.description: random permutation of up to nine tagged words
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(2, 9)
    words = []
    for i in range(k):
        wlen = rng.randint(1, 5)
        w = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_letters) for _ in range(wlen))
        words.append(w + str(i + 1))
    rng.shuffle(words)
    return [' '.join(words)]
